nodes.label: Detailed Status Handling and Fix Flows
nodes.norm_label: detailed status handling and fix flows

nodes.label: Bubbly Minimalism Design Language
nodes.rationale: Design direction combining playful bubbly shapes with minimal layout. Uses DynaPuff headings, Nunito body, rounded-2xl/3xl containers, hard tactile shadows, 60-30-10 color split (background-primary-accent), CSS custom properties (var(--bubbly-*)). Inspired by Blooket, Kahoot, Nintendo Switch UI.

nodes.label: Arcade Energy Aesthetic
nodes.rationale: High-octane visual style: vibrant primary colors, heavy drop shadows mimicking physical arcade buttons, bounce/tactile animations, chunky borders. Makes every interactive element feel pushable. Inspired by 90s arcade cabinets.

nodes.label: Forge Classic Game Mode
nodes.rationale: MVP game mode: host broadcasts questions via WebSocket, players submit answers within time limit, server validates correctness + timestamp, scoring = correctness × speed factor, real-time leaderboard updates. Delivered end-to-end by Sprint 6.

nodes.label: 6-Digit PIN Game Joining
nodes.rationale: Session joining mechanism: host creates session with unique 6-digit PIN, players enter PIN on play page to join lobby. Uniqueness validation ensures no collisions.

nodes.label: Podium-Style Leaderboard
nodes.rationale: Post-game ranking display: 3-step podium with 1st center (highest, gold), 2nd left (silver), 3rd right (bronze). Avatars on top of colored blocks with staggered rise animation. Runner-up list below.

nodes.label: Session State Machine
nodes.rationale: Game session lifecycle: waiting → playing → paused → ended. Controls game flow transitions, host can advance questions, end session broadcasts to all players.

nodes.label: Tactile UI Pattern
nodes.rationale: Neo-Brutalism meets Arcade: 4px solid borders, 8px hard shadows, 16px border radius everywhere. On click, elements translate 4px down-right and shadow reduces to 4px. Creates physical pushable button feel.

nodes.label: Speed-Based Scoring Algorithm
nodes.rationale: Points = Correctness × Speed Factor. Server-side validation with timestamp prevents cheating. Score deltas broadcast to all players in real-time after each question.

nodes.label: Treasure Forge Game Mode
nodes.rationale: Post-MVP game mode: weighted random chest generation after correct answers. Chest contents include gold, multiplier, steal (10% from leader), swap (random player). Gold counter with animations.

nodes.label: Bubbly Royale Game Mode
nodes.rationale: Post-MVP 1v1 duel mode: matchmaking (random or ELO), split-screen UI, life bubble elimination (3 lives), win condition = correct + faster, spectator mode for eliminated players.

nodes.label: Bubbly UI Component Library
nodes.rationale: Shared standalone Angular components: BubblyButton (tone/size/full), BubblyCard (tone/padded/interactive), BubblyInput (CVA form input), BubblySelect (CVA select), BubblyAlert (variant/dismissible), BubblyModal (visible/confirm/danger), PageHeading (eyebrow/title/description), BubblyBadge (count/tone). All use Bubbly Minimalism design tokens.

nodes.label: Service Layer Pattern
nodes.rationale: All routes must use services (not direct DB/Supabase calls) to centralize business logic, authorization, and transaction orchestration. Services sit between controllers and repositories.

nodes.label: Functional Repository Pattern
nodes.rationale: Exported named functions, not classes. Follows the pattern established by user.repository.ts. Consistent across all entities. Repositories handle data access only — no business logic.

nodes.label: Bubbly Minimalism Design Language
nodes.rationale: 60-30-10 color scheme (--bubbly-background, --bubbly-primary, --bubbly-accent), rounded-2xl/3xl corners, DynaPuff headings, Nunito body, solid colors only, no blur/gradients.

nodes.label: Forge Classic Scoring Algorithm
nodes.rationale: Points = Correctness x Speed Factor. Server-side only, deterministic, reusable for leaderboard and analytics. Zero for incorrect answers. Clamps edge cases like negative remaining time.

nodes.label: Session State Machine
nodes.rationale: States: waiting, playing, paused, ended. Transition function enforces valid paths (waiting->playing, playing->paused, paused->playing, playing->ended) and throws InvalidStateTransitionError otherwise.

nodes.label: Zod Validation Schemas
nodes.rationale: Used for DTOs (quiz, session, auth, websocket messages) with conditional validation (superRefine for question options), middleware factories (validateBody, validateParams), and type inference via z.infer.

nodes.label: Socket.IO Room Management
nodes.rationale: Rooms mapped to session PINs. Events: join-game, leave-game, player-joined, player-left. No room leakage between rooms. Disconnect handling for ungraceful teardowns.

nodes.label: Supabase Auth Integration
nodes.rationale: Uses publishable/secret API keys and JWT signing keys (ES256 preferred). authMiddleware verifies Bearer tokens via getClaims() with fallback to getUser(). No legacy JWT secret usage.

nodes.label: Share Code and PIN Utilities
nodes.rationale: Share codes (8-char uppercase alphanumeric, no ambiguous chars, crypto.getRandomValues) identify quizzes for sharing. PINs (6-digit zero-padded numeric) identify sessions for live gameplay. Both have retry logic on collision.

nodes.label: Zoneless Change Detection
nodes.rationale: Angular's experimental zoneless mode (provideExperimentalZonelessChangeDetection). Removes zone.js dependency. Uses Signals for reactivity. Hybrid rendering: SSG for marketing/auth, SSR for public pages, CSR for game engine.
